plan: 1. Create tier filtering pure function in `src/domain/filterCards.ts` — accepts a Card array and a Set<Tier> of active tiers, returns filtered cards. When the tier set is empty, return all cards (no filter active). Export from `src/domain/index.ts`.

2. Create `TierFilter` UI component at `src/ui/TierFilter/TierFilter.tsx` with CSS Module — render three toggle buttons (Open, Working, Deep) styled with existing tier color tokens from `tokens.css`. Each button shows tier label + color indicator (not color-only per accessibility rules). Include a 'Clear filters' button that appears when any tier is selected. Props: `activeTiers: Set<Tier>`, `onToggle: (tier: Tier) => void`, `onClear: () => void`.

3. Wire tier filter state into `src/features/browse/BrowseAllCards.tsx` — add `useState<Set<Tier>>` for active tiers, import `filterByTier` from domain, import `TierFilter` component, apply filter to cardDeck before rendering the grid, pass state + handlers to TierFilter.
4. Update `BrowseAllCards.module.css` to accommodate filter bar above the card grid (toolbar row with margin-bottom).

5. Write unit tests in `tests/unit/filter-cards.test.ts` — test: returns all cards when no tiers selected, filters to Open only, filters to Working only, filters to Deep only, filters to multiple tiers (Open+Deep), returns empty array when no cards match, handles empty input array.

6. Write component tests in `tests/unit/tier-filter.test.tsx` — test: renders three tier buttons, toggling a tier calls onToggle, active tiers are visually indicated, clear button calls onClear, clear button hidden when no tiers active.

7. Update `tests/unit/browse-all-cards.test.tsx` — add tests verifying: tier filter appears on browse page, selecting a tier reduces displayed cards, multiple tiers can be active simultaneously, clearing filters restores all cards.
8. Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to validate all checks pass.
9. Run `npm run test:e2e` (Playwright) to verify browse page still works with filter integration.
testing_recommendations: Unit test filterByTier with each individual tier to confirm Open/Working/Deep filters work independently
Unit test filterByTier with multiple active tiers (e.g., Open+Deep) to confirm multi-select behavior
Unit test filterByTier with empty tier set returns all cards (no-filter baseline)
Component test TierFilter renders all three tier buttons and clear button visibility toggles correctly
Integration test in BrowseAllCards: selecting a tier reduces card count, clearing restores full deck
E2e Playwright test on /browse: click tier buttons, verify card count changes, click clear, verify restoration
Accessibility: verify tier filter buttons are keyboard-navigable and have accessible labels
risks: Tier filter state must remain independent from future category filter state (US ticket notes 'separate from category filters') — avoid coupling the two in shared state
Accessibility: tier buttons must not rely on color alone — ensure text labels are always visible per CLAUDE.md tier system rules
If BrowseAllCards currently has no state, adding useState changes it from a pure display component — straightforward but worth noting for test updates
